LinCS
The System Configuration Tool
   About    News    Docs    Download    Contact   
 

FAQ

Why is tiger having a text interface?

A machine in difficulty might not have a graphic UI. Plus, there are hosts that do not have X11 installed. On the other hand, every host has a console. Another kind of (G)UI can easily be added.

Why is tiger writen in C++?

The best text UI kit available is Turbo Vision, which is written in C++.

Why switch to a new system?

  • One uniform way to store/retrieve configuration values.
  • No more duplicated information.
  • Various benefits: notifications, rollback, etc.
  • Flexible
  • Allows old applications to coexist with the new configuration system - no recompilation needed.

Any disadvantages?

  • The filesystem backend is an inode hog: there is one inode per config entry stored, as there is one file per entry. This can be easily changed by changing the backend with, say, one that stores entries in a flat file or a database.
  • The protocol implies xml parsing and parsing takes time.
  • Connecting to server takes time.
All in all, LinCS is slower than the config files way. This should be no problem though on modern machines.
